etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, 'mouth') + phonetic 今(今) (jīn) -> chant
etymology.interpretation: The character 吟(yín, 'chant') uses the semantic component 口(口) (kǒu, 'mouth') to suggest an action involving the mouth, related to speaking or making sound. The phonetic component 今(今) (jīn) provides the pronunciation. Together, they logically form the concept of 'chanting', which involves producing a melody or rhythm vocally.

examples.chinese: 他常常吟诗(他常常吟詩)。
月光下(月光下)，她的吟声格外动人(她的吟聲格外動人)。
examples.pinyin: Yuèguāng xià, tā de yín shēng géwài dòngrén.
examples.english: Under the moonlight, her chanting was especially moving.

examples.chinese: 老人坐在树下(老人坐在樹下)，低声吟唱着古老的曲调(低聲吟唱著古老的曲調)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎorén zuò zài shù xià, dīshēng yínchàng zhe gǔlǎo de qǔdiào.
examples.english: The old man sat under the tree, softly chanting an ancient tune.
